proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +32.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8; pending review may shift tier
Portfolio regime concentration in Choppy (85.9%) means a regime rotation to Bear could trigger rapid activation of this boost across many fresh entries
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +32.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +32.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with only 11 of 78 Bear exposures, so activation is deferred and the existing Choppy concentration (85.9%) is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-09) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Sample bias not directly verifiable from provided cohort_health — no per-day distribution available; if the 17 trades cluster on few days the Wilson bound overstates confidence
Regime concentration already triggered at 85.9% Choppy; any additional boost surfacing during Choppy compounds dominant-regime risk even with TAKE_SMALL sizing
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI exposure (27 of 78 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-09) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (85.9%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy (especially DOWN_TRI shorts) until regime mix diversifies via natural Day-6 exits or until Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort matures from Candidate to Tier M. Prioritize trimming DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG clusters first if any underperform.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull regime flip simultaneously unwinds 67 Choppy positions including 46 DOWN_TRI shorts with no validated edge backstop; correlated short-squeeze losses across IT and FMCG clusters could compound before Day-6 exit logic clears the book.

evidence: Matched cohort regime=Choppy__signal_type=DOWN_TRI is Candidate tier (n=15) — unvalidated edge
DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 46/78 = 0.590, reinforcing directional skew within unvalidated regime
Top concentration cells DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG both at 0.1026 (below 0.20 cell threshold) but both map to Candidate-tier cohort
Persistent pattern: 7 of last 8 sessions have flagged the same Choppy/DOWN_TRI concentration on Candidate-tier cohort
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy (especially DOWN_TRI shorts) until regime mix diversifies via natural Day-6 exits or until Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort matures from Candidate to Tier M. Prioritize trimming DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG clusters first if any underperform.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull regime flip simultaneously unwinds 67 Choppy positions including 46 DOWN_TRI shorts with no validated edge backstop; correlated short-squeeze losses across IT and FMCG clusters could compound before Day-6 exit logic clears the book.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.859 (Choppy, above 0.80 threshold) and the matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort is only Candidate tier (n=15) — unvalidated edge. No single cell breaches 20% (top cells at 10.26%), but the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn. A Choppy→Bull regime flip would unwind the DOWN_TRI-dominated short book (46/78 = 59%) without statistical justification.
